citation.anchor: Certain exceptions may be deferred rather than generating a fault

preconditions: CPL0 executes with PSR.dt and PSR.ic enabled
A DTC entry maps an aligned WB page whose PTE memory attribute is NaTPage
The .s and .sa destinations contain distinct literal payloads with clear NaTs
Each load uses a distinct base and a positive immediate update equal to one or two datum widths
inputs: Integer widths 1, 2, 4, and 8 bytes
One ldN.s and one ldN.sa per width
One integer chk.s recovery branch for the .s destination
One integer chk.a recovery branch for the .sa destination
expected_results: Each NaTPage reference is deferred without a delivered interruption
Each destination acquires NaT; its 64-bit data portion is implementation-specific
Both immediate base updates retire exactly once despite deferral
CHK.S branches because the .s destination is NaT
CHK.A branches because deferred .sa allocated no ALAT entry
allowed_results: For every width, any two destination data portions plus the exact two updated bases, two NaTs, and two recovery markers are observed with no pending interruption
permitted_state_changes: The implementation-specific data portions and NaT bits of both destinations
Internal translation and ALAT lookup state required by the instructions
required_unchanged_state: The NaTPage backing memory
Unrelated GR, NaT, predicate, and memory state
expected_faults: No delivered fault; Data NaT Page is deferred by both control-speculative load types
cases.committed_prefix: Each speculative load commits only its architecturally required destination NaT and base update; no memory value or ALAT entry is committed for the deferred reference
cases.restart_action: CHK.S and CHK.A each branch to test-owned recovery code; no interruption retry is involved

result_observations: direct typed traces for all loads and checks

oracle.derivation: All widths, addresses, update values, NaT masks, recovery targets, and terminal markers are test-owned literals or direct arithmetic derived from the cited load/check rules; destination data portions are deliberately not compared because Intel defines them as implementation-specific after deferral, and no production translation, ALAT, or exception helper supplies expected values

limitations: This atomic row covers only integer NaTPage deferral at CPL0; other deferrable and non-deferrable fault classes, successful values, FP forms, false predicates, ALAT-hit optionality, and migration remain separate ALT/MEM/MMU rows

preconditions: PSR.ic is enabled and serialized
PSR.it is clear, selecting the no-recovery model regardless of ITLB.ed
PSR.ed is clear and no DCR-based deferral is active
Translated-miss probes enable PSR.dt without installing a matching DTC entry
Alignment probes enable PSR.ac and use either mapped physical RAM or a translated NaTPage whose higher-priority condition always defers
inputs: One ldN.s and one ldN.sa translated-miss probe for widths 1, 2, 4, and 8
One physical and one concurrent-NaTPage ldN.s/ldN.sa misalignment probe for widths 2, 4, and 8
Each destination contains a distinct clear-NaT literal
Each base uses an immediate update equal to its datum width
expected_results: Each qualified Alternate Data TLB condition raises immediately in the no-recovery model
Each qualified Unaligned Data Reference condition raises immediately in the no-recovery model
A higher-priority but always-deferred Data NaT Page condition does not hide the lower non-deferred Unaligned condition
ISR.r and ISR.sp are set while ISR.ed and ISR.ni remain clear
CR.IIP, CR.IFA, the exception record, and the published M slot identify the exact faulting load
No destination, destination NaT, base update, or later instruction effect retires
allowed_results: Exactly the declared Alternate Data TLB or Unaligned Data Reference interruption and unchanged declared destination/base state
required_unchanged_state: The destination data and NaT
The update base
The backing RAM used by alignment probes
Unrelated GR, NaT, predicate, ALAT, and memory state
expected_faults: Alternate Data TLB at vector 0x1000 for every translated-miss probe
Unaligned Data Reference at vector 0x5a00 for every alignment probe
fault_priority: Deferral removes a qualified NaTPage condition from prioritization
Qualified non-deferred alignment remains and raises
Precise interruption collection before any load or update effect
cases.committed_prefix: Only instructions before the faulting load commit; the faulting load commits no destination, NaT, update, ALAT, or memory effect
cases.restart_action: CR.IIP and IPSR.ri identify the faulting M slot for handler retry after software resolves the condition

result_observations: exception kind, vector, source, and address
direct typed trace for every faulting load

oracle.derivation: The no-recovery control state, width matrix, unmapped virtual address, misaligned physical and NaTPage addresses, initial registers, immediate updates, vectors, ISR bits, and non-retirement rules are direct test-owned literals or arithmetic derived from Intel SDM Volume 2 Section 5.5.5 and the interruption definitions; no production translation or speculative-load helper supplies expected values

limitations: This atomic row closes translated misses and PSR.ac alignment conditions, including concurrent always-deferred NaTPage priority, only; page-not-present, key, access-right, access-bit, data-debug, remaining recovery-model controls, always-defer, FP, other simultaneous-condition, retry, and migration matrices remain separate ALT/MEM/MMU work

preconditions: PSR.ic is enabled and serialized while PSR.it and PSR.ed are clear
Each translated protection probe installs one deliberately failing DTC entry
Key probes enable PSR.pk and install explicit valid or read-disabled PKRs
Access-rights probes execute the load at CPL3 against a PL0 page
Data Debug probes enable PSR.db and install an exact PL0 read DBR pair
Data Debug is exercised alone, concurrent with non-deferred Unaligned, and below an always-deferred NaTPage condition
inputs: Page Not Present, Data Key Miss, Key Permission, Data Access Rights, and Data Access Bit for every ld1/2/4/8.s and ld1/2/4/8.sa form
Aligned Data Debug for every width and class
Unaligned-plus-Data-Debug for widths 2, 4, and 8 in both classes
NaTPage-plus-Data-Debug for every width and class
Each destination contains a distinct clear-NaT literal and each base has a width-sized immediate update
expected_results: All 40 translated protection conditions raise their declared fault immediately
All 22 Data Debug conditions raise Data Debug immediately
Data Debug outranks a concurrent non-deferred Unaligned condition
A higher-priority but deferred NaTPage condition does not hide a lower non-deferred Data Debug condition
ISR.r and ISR.sp are set while ISR.ed and ISR.ni remain clear
CR.IIP, CR.IFA, CR.IPSR, the exception record, and the published M slot identify the exact faulting load and active controls
No destination value, destination NaT, immediate base update, or later instruction effect retires
allowed_results: Exactly the declared Page Not Present, Data Key Miss, Key Permission, Data Access Rights, Data Access Bit, or Data Debug interruption with unchanged destination and base state
permitted_state_changes: Internal failed translation, protection-key, and breakpoint lookup state
required_unchanged_state: The destination data and NaT
The update base
Unrelated GR, NaT, predicate, and memory state
expected_faults: Page Not Present at vector 0x5000
Data Key Miss at vector 0x1c00
Key Permission at vector 0x5100
Data Access Rights at vector 0x5300
Data Access Bit at vector 0x2800
Data Debug at vector 0x5900
fault_priority: Data Debug before Unaligned when both remain non-deferred
Deferred NaTPage removed before lower Data Debug prioritization
Precise interruption collection before any load or update effect
cases.committed_prefix: Only instructions before the faulting load commit; the faulting load commits no destination, NaT, update, ALAT, or memory effect
cases.restart_action: CR.IIP and IPSR.ri identify the faulting M slot for handler repair and retry

result_observations: exception kind, vector, source, and address
direct typed trace for every faulting load

oracle.derivation: Every PTE, ITIR key, PKR permission bit, CPL, DBR pair, address, width, initial register, update, vector, ISR bit, and non-retirement result is a test-owned literal or direct arithmetic derived from Intel SDM Volume 2 page-access, debug, and qualified-deferral rules; no production translation, key, debug, or speculative-load helper supplies expected values

limitations: This row closes the five selected DCR-controlled protection classes and Data Debug in the no-recovery model only; recovery-model DCR protection/debug deferral, always-defer, PSR.ed, FP, other simultaneous conditions, handler retry, and migration remain separate ALT/MMU work

citation.anchor: If additional TLB misses are encountered during the VHPT access, a VHPT Translation fault is raised

preconditions: PSR.ic and PSR.dt are enabled and serialized while PSR.it and PSR.ed are clear
Region zero uses RR.ve=1, RR.ps=12, and a 32-KiB short-format VHPT with PTA.vf=0 and PTA.size=15
The original virtual address has no DTC translation
Invalid-entry probes map the IHA backing page but place the literal invalid short-format entry 0x3 at IHA
Missing-backing probes leave the IHA page itself absent from the DTC
inputs: One invalid-entry and one missing-backing probe for every ld1/2/4/8.s and ld1/2/4/8.sa form
The original virtual address is 0x8000, producing literal short-format IHA 0x40
Each destination contains a distinct clear-NaT literal and each base has a width-sized immediate update
expected_results: A mapped but invalid short-format VHPT entry raises Data TLB immediately at vector 0x0800
A DTC miss while the walker references IHA raises VHPT Translation immediately at vector 0x0000
CR.IFA retains the original address while CR.IHA contains the walk address and CR.ITIR contains RR.ps=12 with RID zero
ISR.r and ISR.sp are set while ISR.ed and ISR.ni remain clear
CR.IIP, the exception record, and the published M slot identify the exact load
No destination, destination NaT, immediate base update, or later program effect retires
allowed_results: Exactly Data TLB for the invalid entry or VHPT Translation for missing walker backing, with the declared interruption registers and unchanged destination/base state
permitted_state_changes: Internal failed translation and VHPT lookup state
required_unchanged_state: The destination data and NaT
The update base
The invalid VHPT backing word
Unrelated GR, NaT, predicate, ALAT, and memory state
expected_faults: Data TLB at vector 0x0800 for every invalid short-format entry
VHPT Translation at vector 0x0000 for every missing walker-backing translation
fault_priority: No-recovery delivery before any load or update effect
cases.committed_prefix: Only instructions before the faulting load commit; the load commits no destination, NaT, update, ALAT, or memory effect
cases.restart_action: The handler uses IHA and ITIR to map the walker backing page or uses IFA to resolve the absent leaf before retrying the exact CR.IIP M slot

result_observations: exception kind, vector, source, and original address
CR.IIP, CR.IFA, CR.ISR, CR.IHA, and CR.ITIR
direct typed trace for every faulting load

oracle.derivation: The RR, PTA, short-format index arithmetic, backing translation, invalid leaf word, width matrix, addresses, initial registers, updates, vectors, ISR bits, ITIR, and non-retirement rules are test-owned literals or direct arithmetic from the cited Intel short-VHPT, interruption, and qualified-deferral rules; no production translation, VHPT, or speculative-load helper supplies expected values

limitations: This row closes short-format VHPT Data and Data TLB selection in the no-recovery model only; long-format VHPT, successful walker insertion, always-defer, PSR.ed, FP, handler repair/retry, and migration remain separate ALT/MMU work

The selected data address produces Alternate Data TLB, mapped-invalid-leaf Data TLB, or missing-walker-backing VHPT Data
PSR.ed is clear
inputs: Every ld1/2/4/8.s and ld1/2/4/8.sa form
Three translation fault selections

Exactly the 24 ITLB.ed=0/DCR.dm=1 combinations raise their selected fault with ISR.r and ISR.sp set but ISR.ed clear
Immediate faults retain the original destination data, clear NaT, and unmodified base
VHPT-selected immediate faults publish exact original IFA, derived IHA, and default ITIR

Alternate Data TLB 0x1000, Data TLB 0x0800, or VHPT Translation 0x0000 when either conjunction input is zero
fault_priority: Deferral to NaT/check recovery or precise fault before load effects
cases.committed_prefix: Deferred loads commit only destination NaT with implementation-specific data and the exact base update; immediate loads commit no destination, NaT, update, ALAT, or later effect
cases.restart_action: Deferred .s and .sa transfer through CHK.S and absent-entry CHK.A recovery; immediate handlers may use ISR.ed to decide software deferral or repair and retry CR.IIP

citation.anchor: When PSR.ic is 0 and regardless of the state of DCR

preconditions: PSR.ic, PSR.it, PSR.ed, and every DCR deferral bit are clear and serialized
Each translated probe enables PSR.dt and independently constructs exactly one qualified Table 5-4 condition whose deferral equation contains !PSR.ic
Key, CPL3, DBR, PSR.db, and PSR.ac controls are enabled only for their corresponding condition
CR.DCR is read back as zero before the possible CPL3 transition
inputs: Every ld1/2/4/8.s and ld1/2/4/8.sa form for Alternate Data TLB, VHPT Data, Data TLB, Page Not Present, Data Key Miss, Key Permission, Data Access Rights, Data Access Bit, and Data Debug
Every naturally alignable ld2/4/8.s and ld2/4/8.sa form for Unaligned Data Reference
A width-sized immediate base update and distinct clear-NaT destination
CHK.S for deferred .s and absent-entry CHK.A for deferred .sa
expected_results: All 78 qualified references defer without delivering an interruption solely because PSR.ic is zero
Every destination becomes NaT and every width-sized immediate base update retires exactly once
CHK.S branches on each .s NaT and CHK.A branches because each deferred .sa load allocated no ALAT entry
PSR.ic remains zero while each condition-specific PSR control remains active
CR.DCR remains exactly zero

permitted_state_changes: The deferred destination data portion and NaT
Internal translation, VHPT, key, debug, and ALAT lookup state
required_unchanged_state: CR.DCR
The test-owned backing memory and invalid VHPT leaf

citation.anchor: When PSR.ed is 1, then a deferred exception indicator

preconditions: RFI makes a p0-qualified integer .s or .sa load the first restarted instruction with IPSR.ed=1
PSR.ic=1, PSR.it=0, and CR.DCR=0 isolate software-forced deferral from PSR.ic and recovery-model hardware deferral
Test-owned physical or translated state constructs every integer-applicable Table 5-4 condition plus one valid WB reference
A second safe WB base is available for the same load form after checked recovery
inputs: Every ld1/2/4/8.s and ld1/2/4/8.sa form across a valid WB reference and Register NaT, Unimplemented Data Address, Alternate Data TLB, VHPT Data, Data TLB, Page Not Present, Data NaT Page, Data Key Miss, Key Permission, Data Access Rights, Data Access Bit, and Data Debug
Every ld2/4/8.s and ld2/4/8.sa form for Unaligned Data Reference
A width-sized immediate base update on the forced load
CHK.S for forced .s and absent-entry CHK.A for forced .sa
A no-update safe load of the same form after recovery
expected_results: All 110 first restarted loads write destination NaT without delivering an interruption or performing their data reference
Every width-sized post-increment retires, including the NaT base while preserving its base NaT
CHK.S or CHK.A takes recovery, and the pre-existing exact success-case .sa ALAT entry is purged
PSR.ed clears after the forced instruction
The following safe load executes normally, returns the exact width-selected WB value, and leaves its destination NaT clear
PSR.ic and condition-specific controls remain active while CR.DCR remains zero
allowed_results: The forced destination data portion is implementation-specific; its NaT, exact base update and base NaT, checked recovery, ED consumption, exact subsequent safe value, controls, and absence of interruption are mandatory
permitted_state_changes: The forced destination data portion and NaT
The safe-load destination and any new safe .sa ALAT entry
required_unchanged_state: CR.DCR
The test-owned backing memory and invalid VHPT leaf
Unrelated GR, NaT, predicate, and memory state
expected_faults: No delivered fault from the forced load or the following safe load
fault_priority: First-restart PSR.ed action before address NaT, translation, protection, debug, alignment, or data access
Normal execution of the following load
cases.committed_prefix: The forced load commits only destination NaT with implementation-specific data, its exact post-increment, corresponding .sa ALAT purge, and ED consumption; it performs no data reference
cases.restart_action: RFI supplies IPSR.ed for exactly the restarted load; checked recovery continues after the forced deferred indicator, and the next load is not software-deferred

result_observations: forced destination NaT and unconstrained data portion
exact base update and base NaT
exact following safe-load value and clear NaT
condition-specific PSR controls and zero DCR
direct typed traces for the forced load, check, and safe load

citation.anchor: Register Return Values on Non-faulting Advanced/Speculative Loads

preconditions: PSR.ic=1 and PSR.ed=0 with no exception condition
A translated WB mapping selects the speculative class, physical WBL selects limited speculation, and a translated UC mapping selects the non-speculative class
Every check-load hit probe first executes an exact same-register/address/size advanced load; every miss probe begins with an empty ALAT
Non-speculative check-load hit behavior is excluded because Intel defines it as undefined
inputs: Every ld1/2/4/8.s, ld1/2/4/8.a, and ld1/2/4/8.sa form across all three speculation classes
Every ld1/2/4/8.c.clr, ld1/2/4/8.c.clr.acq, and ld1/2/4/8.c.nc form across exact hit and clean miss on speculative and limited pages
Every integer clear, acquire-clear, and no-clear check-load miss on a non-speculative page
A width-sized immediate base update and width-selected nonzero backing value
CHK.S or CHK.A records the guest-visible deferred-token or ALAT-check edge
expected_results: All 36 Table 4-14 load/class/attribute combinations return the exact value, zero, or destination NaT required by the table and retire the exact update
Speculative .s falls through CHK.S; limited and non-speculative .s branch on NaT
Speculative .a and .sa plus limited .a return the exact width-selected value with clear NaT; non-speculative .a returns zero; limited/non-speculative .sa return NaT
All 60 Table 4-17 check-load combinations return the exact width-selected value with clear NaT and retire the exact update

Clear-form clean misses and every non-speculative miss leave no entry; no-clear misses on speculative/limited pages and every exact-hit check honor the architecture-permitted subsequent CHK.A hit-or-pessimistic-failure set
No interruption is delivered
allowed_results: An implementation may fail CHK.A or an ALAT lookup even when an exact entry exists, so both check edges are accepted only where Intel permits pessimistic failure
Destination data after a control-speculation failure is implementation-specific and is checked only through NaT
All exact values, zero results, NaT states, updates, mandatory no-entry cases, and absence of interruption are required
permitted_state_changes: The declared load/check destinations and NaT bits
ALAT allocation, replacement, pessimistic lookup failure, and clear behavior within the cited permitted set
required_unchanged_state: The test-owned backing memory
Unrelated GR, NaT, predicate, translation, and memory state
expected_faults: No delivered fault for any of the 96 non-faulting speculation-attribute cases
fault_priority: Speculation class selected by the memory attribute
Table 4-14 load response or Table 4-17 ALAT lookup response
cases.committed_prefix: Each qualified instruction commits exactly its table-selected destination/NaT result and post-increment; check-load and advanced-load ALAT effects remain within Intel's explicit optional lookup and replacement set
cases.restart_action: No interruption restart occurs; CHK.S exposes successful or failed control speculation and CHK.A exposes a permitted hit or pessimistic failure

result_observations: exact width-selected value or advanced-load zero
direct typed traces for every load and check

oracle.derivation: All 96 programs, PTE memory attributes, physical WBL selection, widths, classes, hit/miss preconditions, backing values, updates, and allowed result sets are test-owned literals or direct arithmetic from Intel SDM Volume 2 Tables 4-13, 4-14, and 4-17 plus Volume 1 section 4.4.5.3.1; no production translation, load, or ALAT helper supplies expected values

limitations: This row closes non-faulting integer value/NaT/update and exact-match/clean-miss behavior for one representative attribute in each architectural speculation class; WC/UCE attribute identity, partial ALAT matches, aliases, invalidation sources, false predicates, acquire ordering, FP forms, spontaneous replacement stress, migration, and malformed ALAT state remain separate MEM/ALT/ATM work

citation.anchor: same register as their corresponding advanced load

preconditions: Translated WB data selects the speculative ALAT class
Each advanced load uses a test-owned aligned address, size, and GR or FR target
GR16 is loaded in bank 0 before BSW.1 presents the distinct bank-1 physical register
Mismatched clear checks use valid backing data so their architecturally undefined destination is never used as an oracle
inputs: LD1/2/4/8.A into r20 followed by CHK.A of distinct r21
One GR-to-FR and one FR-to-GR same-number type-tag probe
LD1/2/4/8.C.CLR with a different address or a different width from its corresponding advanced load
LD8.A r16 in bank 0, BSW.1, and CHK.A r16 in bank 1
expected_results: Checks of a distinct GR tag or target type take recovery
The bank-0 r16 entry cannot satisfy a bank-1 r16 check
Every mismatched clear check invalidates the matching register tag independently of address or size, so a following CHK.A takes recovery
BSW.0 restores the original bank-0 loaded value
allowed_results: An exact source entry may be pessimistically evicted before any check
The destination of an address-, size-, or type-mismatched check load is undefined and is not inspected
No distinct-target, distinct-type, or distinct-physical-bank false hit is permitted
permitted_state_changes: Declared GR or FR advanced-load destinations
ALAT allocation, pessimistic eviction, target-tag clear, and bank-transition eviction
required_unchanged_state: Test-owned backing data except where no store occurs
Unrelated GR, FR, NaT, predicate, and translation state
Bank-0 r16 data while bank 1 is selected
expected_faults: No delivered interruption
cases.committed_prefix: Advanced loads may allocate or pessimistically lose their entry; distinct tags never create a false hit, and a clear check removes any same-register tag before the following observation
cases.restart_action: No interruption restart occurs; CHK.A recovery exposes mandatory absence without relying on an optional successful lookup

oracle.derivation: Targets, GR/FR types, BSW encodings, addresses, widths, mismatch pairs, branch edges, and allowed pessimistic-failure set are test-owned literals derived from Intel SDM Volume 1 sections 4.4.5.3.1 and 4.4.5.5; no production ALAT structure or matching helper supplies expected results

limitations: Physical memory aliases, stacked and rotating register aliases, same-target replacement stress, FP widths beyond the representative single form, and migration of live entries remain separate ALT-004/ALT-007 work

citation.anchor: ALAT entries whose memory regions overlap

preconditions: Translated WB advanced loads establish test-owned GR or FR target tags
Ordinary stores and successful XCHG operations address the exact advanced-load byte range
INVALA starts with two represented GR tags
INVALA.E selects two GR tags or two FR tags in sequence
inputs: Overlapping ST1/2/4/8 after matching-width LD1/2/4/8.A
Successful XCHG1/2/4/8 after matching-width LD1/2/4/8.A
INVALA with two live GR targets
GR and FR forms of INVALA.E for two selected targets
expected_results: Every store commits the exact width-selected value and its following CHK.A misses
Every XCHG returns the exact old value, stores the exact new value, and its following CHK.A misses
INVALA forces misses for both represented tags
Each INVALA.E forces a miss for its selected GR or FR tag
allowed_results: Unrelated entries may be pessimistically invalidated
An entry may already be absent before an explicit invalidator
No overlapped or explicitly selected entry may remain observably valid afterward
permitted_state_changes: Declared memory bytes and atomic destinations
ALAT entries overlapped or selected by the operation plus permitted pessimistic evictions
required_unchanged_state: Memory outside each declared store or atomic width
Unrelated architectural registers, predicates, NaTs, and translation state
expected_faults: No delivered interruption
fault_priority: Enabled predicate and legal aligned operands
ALAT invalidation no later than store visibility
cases.committed_prefix: Each local store or successful semaphore commits once and invalidates every overlapping represented range; explicit invalidators remove their declared scope before later checks
cases.restart_action: No interruption restart occurs; following CHK.A recovery exposes mandatory absence

oracle.derivation: Widths, addresses, initial and replacement values, explicit invalidator encodings, target types, and recovery edges are test-owned literals derived from Intel SDM Volume 1 section 4.4.5.3.2; no production overlap or invalidation helper supplies the expected outcome

limitations: Partial overlap offsets, failed compare-exchange, RSE traffic, register/memory context changes, interruption transitions, remote coherence agents, cache removal, and ptc.ga remain separate ALT-005 work

citation.anchor: test of the qualifying predicate is included

preconditions: A compare sets nonzero p1 true and its complement p2 false
True-predicate cases use a translated speculative WB mapping
False-predicate cases use an unimplemented data address that would fault if referenced
Destinations, NaTs, and bases begin with test-owned sentinels
inputs: LD1/2/4/8.A and LD1/2/4/8.SA with p1 true
The same eight forms with p2 false
A width-sized immediate base update
A following unpredicated CHK.A
expected_results: Every p1 form loads the exact width-selected value, clears destination NaT, and retires the exact update
Every p2 form performs no memory reference, raises no fault, preserves destination/NaT/base, and creates no ALAT entry
Following CHK.A must recover for every false case; true cases accept Intel's permitted hit or pessimistic miss
allowed_results: A true qualified advanced-load entry may be pessimistically evicted before CHK.A
False qualification permits only the exact no-effect result
permitted_state_changes: p1 and p2 from the test-owned compare
Declared destination, NaT, base update, and optional ALAT entry only in true cases
required_unchanged_state: False-case destination, NaT, base, memory, and ALAT state
Unrelated GR, NaT, predicate, translation, and memory state
expected_faults: No delivered interruption, including from the false-qualified unimplemented address
fault_priority: Only if true: legal operands and address processing
Only if true: data access, destination/update retirement, and ALAT allocation
cases.committed_prefix: A false-qualified advanced load commits nothing; a true-qualified load commits its exact value, NaT clear, base update, and permitted ALAT allocation
cases.restart_action: No interruption restart occurs; CHK.A exposes mandatory absence for false qualification

oracle.derivation: Predicate production, p1/p2 selection, unimplemented address, sentinels, widths, values, updates, and recovery edges are test-owned encodings and literals derived from Intel SDM Volume 3 predication rules and Volume 1 ALAT allocation rules; no production predicate or load helper supplies expected results

limitations: False-predicated floating advanced loads, check loads, explicit invalidators, faulted true advanced loads beyond already claimed rows, and same-group forwarded predicates remain separate ALT/MEM coverage
